summary: Full-stack testing of AI Lead Verification CRM. All 20 backend API tests pass after fixing 2 critical 500 bugs. All frontend pages load and work correctly. Super admin and business owner flows tested end-to-end.

critical.issue: 500 Internal Server Error - pymongo insert_one mutates doc adding ObjectId _id field, causing JSON serialization failure. Fixed by adding doc.pop('_id', None) before returning.
Same ObjectId serialization bug as leads. Fixed.

issues: Demo credentials shown in plain text on login page - minor security concern

action_items: Consider removing demo credentials from login page UI for production
Brute force lockout logic has potential timezone handling edge case in locked_until comparison (auth.py line 131) - works but could be simplified
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: POST /api/leads/ and POST /api/appointments/ - pymongo insert_one mutates doc adding _id ObjectId, causing 500 on return. Pattern: always call doc.pop('_id', None) after insert_one before spreading doc in response.
Minor: Brute force lockout in auth.py line 131 has complex timezone comparison - should be simplified
Minor: seed_admin in startup only creates admin if not exists (idempotent), but update branch only updates if password changed - correct behavior

success_rate.frontend: 100% - all pages load and function correctly

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_owner_abc123@example.com business owner and TEST Business ABC during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both critical ObjectId serialization bugs are fixed. All 20 backend tests pass. Frontend tested: login (admin+owner), register, dashboard, leads (Add Lead), appointments (Book Appointment), conversations, analytics, chatbot, settings, billing, integrations, logout - all working. Mocked: Twilio SMS (demo mode), Stripe (test key).

rca_of_issues.root_cause: Python pymongo's insert_one() mutates the passed dict by adding '_id' (ObjectId) field in-place. When the response does {id: appt_id, **doc}, the _id ObjectId is included which is not JSON serializable by FastAPI's default JSONResponse.
rca_of_issues.fix: Added doc.pop('_id', None) after insert_one() before returning the spread doc in both leads.py and appointments.py
rca_of_issues.reproduction: POST /api/leads/ with valid cookie and body {name, phone} would return 500
